title: diemisa capitata
parts: Diemisa capitata has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of the Diemisa capitata plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and saponins, which are used to treat digestive issues and fever. The flowers of Diemisa capitata contain coumarins and phenolic compounds, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Diemisa capitata can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea and vomiting, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may cause dizziness, headaches, and stomach cramps. In severe cases, diemisa capitata can interact with other medications, resulting in adverse reactions such as rapid heartbeat and increased blood pressure. Furthermore, using diemisa capitata without proper guidance can lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching.
